days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring the vibrant streets

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a hearty breakfast at Australia Dairy Company. Famous for its Hong Kong-style breakfast sets, enjoy toast, scrambled eggs, and milk tea. This local favorite offers a vibrant dining atmosphere with fast service.

timeline.title: Take the MTR to Central Station
timeline.detail: Hop on the MTR from your hotel to Central Station, where you can explore the city's historic and modern architecture. The trains are efficient and provide a convenient way to navigate the bustling urban landscape.

timeline.title: 📸 Photospot: Victoria Peak
timeline.detail: Victoria Peak offers stunning panoramic views of Hong Kong's skyline, harbor, and hills. It's a perfect location for capturing the city's beauty. The vibrant lights are best captured in the early evening.
timeline.tip: Best angle: stand near the viewing platform. Shoot toward the harbor. Best light: late afternoon/golden hour. Include some greenery in the foreground.

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Enjoy authentic wonton noodles at Mak's Noodle, a legendary spot known for its rich flavors and handmade wontons. This small eatery is perfect for indulging in traditional Hong Kong cuisine.

timeline.title: Exploration of Central District
timeline.detail: After lunch, stroll through Central District to appreciate its blend of history and modernity. Explore landmarks like the Man Mo Temple and the vibrant street art, immersing yourself in local culture and history.

timeline.title: Star Ferry Ride
timeline.detail: Experience the iconic Star Ferry ride across Victoria Harbour. This short trip provides a unique perspective of Hong Kong's skyline and is a great way to relax while enjoying the sea breeze.

timeline.title: Street Food Adventure at Mong Kok
timeline.detail: Head to Mong Kok for an exciting street food adventure. Try local delicacies like egg waffles, curry fish balls, and stinky tofu while wandering through the bustling streets filled with shops and local atmosphere.

timeline.title: Check-in at Hotel ICON
timeline.detail: Check in to Hotel ICON, a luxurious accommodation with stunning views of Victoria Harbour and a rooftop pool. The hotel offers modern rooms and excellent amenities, ensuring a comfortable stay for your trip.

timeline.title: Dinner at DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Store
timeline.detail: Conclude your day with dinner at DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Store, where you can experience various authentic dim sum dishes. This restaurant is popular for its fresh ingredients and delicious flavors, providing a perfect end to your day.
timeline.mapQuery: DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Store, Hong Kong

localTips: Buy an Octopus card at the airport for convenient public transport.
Try local dim sum in the morning at Tim Ho Wan.
Visit Victoria Peak at sunset for the best views of the city.

days.title: Cultural Discoveries
days.subtitle: History and local flavors

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a delicious Hong Kong-style breakfast at Australia Dairy Company. Known for its classic breakfast meals, try the scrambled eggs, toast, and milk tea for a local experience.

timeline.title: Transport to Hong Kong Museum of History
timeline.detail: Take the MTR from Jordan Station to Tsim Sha Tsui Station. This ride will take approximately 5 minutes and is an easy route to accessing one of the local cultural highlights.

timeline.title: Hong Kong Museum of History
timeline.detail: Explore the rich history of Hong Kong at the Hong Kong Museum of History. From ancient artifacts to modern exhibits, discover the stories that shaped this vibrant city.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong Museum of History, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Transport to Kowloon Walled City Park
timeline.detail: Walk from the museum to Kowloon Walled City Park, which takes around 15 minutes. Enjoy the scenery as you transition through the streets filled with local life.
timeline.mapQuery: Kowloon Walled City Park, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Kowloon Walled City Park
timeline.detail: Visit Kowloon Walled City Park, a historical park that was once a fortified military outpost. Enjoy the gardens, ponds, and remnants of the city’s fascinating past.
timeline.mapQuery: Kowloon Walled City Park, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Experience dim sum at Tim Ho Wan, known as the world's cheapest Michelin-star restaurant. Indulge in their famous baked buns and prawn dumplings for an authentic taste.

timeline.title: Transport back to Tsim Sha Tsui
timeline.detail: Return to Tsim Sha Tsui on the MTR, enjoying a 15-minute ride back to the vibrant area for your evening activities.
timeline.mapQuery: MTR Tsim Sha Tsui Station, Hong Kong

timeline.title: 📸 Photospot: Victoria Harbour Promenade
timeline.detail: Capture stunning views of the skyline and the harbor. This spot is perfect for evening photography when the lights begin to twinkle over the water.
timeline.tip: Best angle: stand by the water's edge. Shoot toward the skyline. Best light: golden hour for warm tones.

timeline.title: Temple Street Night Market
timeline.detail: Explore the bustling Temple Street Night Market. Sample street food, browse local goods, and soak in the vibrant atmosphere filled with enthusiastic vendors and local shoppers.
timeline.mapQuery: Temple Street Night Market, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Sing Heung Yuen
timeline.detail: End your day enjoying a hearty bowl of tomato noodles at Sing Heung Yuen, a popular local eatery known for its unique flavors and authentic atmosphere.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel
timeline.detail: After a fulfilling day of cultural discoveries, take a leisurely walk back to your hotel, reflecting on the experiences and sights of the day.

localTips: Plan your visit to the Hong Kong Museum of History in the morning to avoid crowds.
Explore Temple Street Night Market for great street food and shopping.
Use the MTR service map for easy navigation through the city.

days.title: Final Explorations
days.subtitle: Last day in the bustling city
highlights: Ferry ride to Tsim Sha Tsui
localTips: Head to Mong Kok early to enjoy the street markets and avoid crowds.
Book your ferry tickets to Tsim Sha Tsui in advance if you plan to take a night cruise.
Visit Man Mo Temple for a cultural experience — it's free and features beautiful architecture.
